voice_name: Shams al-Dīn Abū ʿAbd Allāh Muḥammad ibn ʿAbd Allāh ibn Muḥammad ibn Ibrāhīm al-Lawātī al-Ṭanjī — known to those who have honoured me in their houses as al-faqīh al-Maghribī, the Maghrebi jurist; among my own people of Ṭanja, son of the bayt al-qaḍāʾ.

1.selected_form: A halt at Delhi — one scenic cell, the durbār of Tughluq remembered, with the matter brought before me folded in as the qāḍī folds a ruling into an anecdote.
1.stance: The qāḍī's grave courtesy with the laugh of the road behind it — measured indignation at the conversion of ledger into rule, sealed without strain by the seven-times-rehearsed ruling of the Maghribi bench.
